CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE test_table_2 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_table_2 (id, description) VALUES (1, 'Sample Description 1');
INSERT INTO test_table_2 (id, description) VALUES (2, 'Sample Description 2');
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED;
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ COMMITTED;
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id in (SELECT id FROM test_table_2 WHERE description LIKE '%Sample%');
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'Charlie' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE name = 'Alice';
SELECT id, name FROM test_table JOIN test_table_2 ON test_table.id = test_table_2.id;
